title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jules, Gwendolyn, and Miss Marple discuss three potential suspects in the murder of Helen Hardy.

The first suspect is Erskine. Jules reconstructs a theory that Erskine, who possessed an attractive quality toward women, met Helen on the beach. He suggests Erskine may have insisted she run away with him, and when she refused, he strangled her in a rage. Jules posits that Erskine then buried the body and attempted to frame Kelvin Hardy. This theory is supported by the fact that Helen had expressed fear of a man and that Erskine admitted to seeing her again.

The second suspect is Walter Feen. Gwendolyn describes Feen as a repressed, obsessive man who was deeply hurt when Helen abandoned him in India to marry Kelvin Hardy. Although he appeared gentle, he had a history of violent rage as a child. Helen had reportedly told him, "I've always been afraid of you," and she had planned to move to Norfolk to escape him. Jules notes that Feen lived within walking distance of the scene and suggests he might have killed her to avoid a scandal if she changed her mind about running away with him.

The third suspect is Jackie Averycliff. Gwendolyn notes that Averycliff suffered from paranoia and held a grudge against Helen following a failed fling, also blaming Feen for losing his job. Jules suggests Averycliff may have been Helen's secret lover, a theory supported by Lily Kimball’s letter mentioning a fancy car parked outside that night, which was identified as Averycliff's. Jules posits that if Helen decided not to run away with him, Averycliff might have killed her and taken her suitcase and letters to make it appear she had fled abroad.
